front: Figure: the first Prime Minister of independent India
Figure: the father of future Prime Minister Indira Gandhi
Event: this leader's birthday, November 14th, is celebrated in India as the holiday Children's Day (Bal Divas)
Event: gave this 1947 inaugural speech declaring India would "redeem its pledge" and "awaken to life and freedom" at the stroke of the midnight hour
work: "Tryst with Destiny" and "The Light Has Gone Out of Our Lives"

front: Figure: lamented "the light has gone out of our lives, and there is darkness everywhere" after the assassination of his mentor Mahatma Gandhi by Nathuram Godse
work: "Tryst with Destiny" and "The Light Has Gone Out of Our Lives"

front: Work: this book, written in prison as letters to his daughter, outlines world history and praises figures like Lenin
Work: this collection of educational letters was addressed to his young daughter Indira
back: Letters from a Father to His Daughter (Jawaharlal Nehru)

front: Work: written in prison at Ahmednagar Fort, this book discusses the Vedas, the Indus Valley civilization, and British colonization
Figure: published an article denouncing his own rise to power, titled "We want no Caesars," under the pseudonym "Chanakya"
Institution: this plan established technical institutes that later became the IITs (National Institutes of Technology)
work: Domestic Reforms: Five Year Plans, States Reorganization, and the License Raj

front: Institution: this commission, headed by Fazal Ali, reorganized India's states along linguistic lines
work: Domestic Reforms: Five Year Plans, States Reorganization, and the License Raj

front: Institution: this system of regulations and nationalizations grew out of Nehru's socialist economic program
work: Domestic Reforms: Five Year Plans, States Reorganization, and the License Raj

front: Figure: wrote his concept of "scientific temper" into India's constitution
work: Domestic Reforms: Five Year Plans, States Reorganization, and the License Raj

front: Event: this military operation annexed the princely state of Hyderabad after its Nizam refused to join India
Event: oversaw the accession of 565 princely states to India, aided by Home Minister Sardar Patel's persuasion
Figure: imprisoned his former friend Sheikh Abdullah over alleged separatist plotting in Kashmir and installed Bakshi Ghulam Mohammed as its leader instead
Institution: this movement, co-founded by Josip Broz Tito, built on Nehru's Panchsheel (Five Principles of Peaceful Coexistence) treaty with China
work: Foreign Policy: Non-Alignment and the Panchsheel

front: Figure: offered political asylum to the Dalai Lama during China's invasion of Tibet
work: Foreign Policy: Non-Alignment and the Panchsheel

front: Figure: a diplomat representing this leader's India gave the longest recorded speech at the UN Security Council, speaking for eight hours until collapsing from exhaustion
work: Foreign Policy: Non-Alignment and the Panchsheel

front: Institution: this border policy, establishing forts along the disputed Chinese border, helped provoke the 1962 Sino-Indian War
work: The Forward Policy and the 1962 Sino-Indian War

front: Figure: the Henderson Brooks report found his military underprepared for the 1962 war; a jeep procurement scandal forced this leader's defense minister V.K. Menon to resign
work: The Forward Policy and the 1962 Sino-Indian War

front: Figure: failed to reach a compromise with Muhammad Ali Jinnah, contributing to the 1947 Partition
work: The Forward Policy and the 1962 Sino-Indian War

front: Figure: his signature collarless coat was popularized in the West in the 1960s by groups like the Monkees and the Beatles
Figure: told physicist Homi Bhabha to "take care of physics" while he handled international relations
Figure: as a leader of the Indian National Congress, supported the revolutionary Bhagat Singh's prison hunger strike
